---
title: National Museum of American History
author: Smithsonian
format: html
image: https://americanhistory.si.edu/sites/default/files/styles/page_hero/public/blog_teaser_images/LGBT_Buttons_2.JPG?itok=xflUkZvG
image-alt: "Buttons reading How Dare You Presume I'm Heterosexual, Come out, and Closets are for Cloths"
---

[Better Gay than Grumpy](https://americanhistory.si.edu/explore/stories/better-gay-grumpy)

![LGBT Buttons - National Museum of American History](https://americanhistory.si.edu/sites/default/files/styles/page_hero/public/blog_teaser_images/LGBT_Buttons_2.JPG)

![LGBT Buttons - National Museum of American History](https://americanhistory.si.edu/sites/default/files/styles/blog_image/public/LGBT_Buttons_1.JPG?itok=9R4MEyc7 "Three buttons reading Better Gay than Grumpy and My Unicorn is a Lesbian. Is Yours? and I'm Straight. But not Narrow")


[Spinsters, confirmed bachelors, and LGBTQ collecting](https://americanhistory.si.edu/explore/stories/spinsters-confirmed-bachelors-and-lgbtq-collecting)

![Transgender flag designed by Monica Helms (right), and friends. The flag's stripes represent the traditional pink and blue associated with girls and boys and white for intersex, transitioning, or of undefined gender. Helms served in the United States Navy and became an activist for transgender rights in the late 1990s in Arizona where she grew up. She designed the flag in 1999.](https://americanhistory.si.edu/sites/default/files/blog_files/a/6a00e553a80e10883401a511f8db61970c-500wi.png "Transgender flag designed by Monica Helms (right), and friends. The flag's stripes represent the traditional pink and blue associated with girls and boys and white for intersex, transitioning, or of undefined gender. Helms served in the United States Navy and became an activist for transgender rights in the late 1990s in Arizona where she grew up. She designed the flag in 1999.")



[A look back at LGBTQ+ rights](https://americanhistory.si.edu/blog/2013/06/new-objects-in-the-collection-represent-landmark-lawrence-v-texas-decision-look-back-at-lgbt-rights.html)

[New Americans and the inspirational lady in New York harbor](https://americanhistory.si.edu/explore/stories/new-americans)

![The Immokalee Statue of Liberty - Coalición de Trabajadores de Immokalee o CIW - Smithsonian](https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/NMAH-JN2015-8136.jpg "Statue of Liberty “holding a tomato in her right hand instead of a torch, and a basket of tomatoes in her left hand instead of a tablet. Esta estatua de la libertad, hecha por la Coalición de Trabajadores de Immokalee o CIW (por sus siglas en ingles) en 2000, fue llevada a una marcha de 230 millas por la dignidad, el diálogo y un salario justo.")


![Refugees Crossing the Border Wall into South Texas by Rigoberto Gonzalez - Smithsonian](https://npr.brightspotcdn.com/dims3/default/strip/false/crop/2477x2910+0+0/resize/1200/quality/85/format/webp/?url=http%3A%2F%2Fnpr-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F65%2F4f%2F71a0629d42c38abf6f007db187a6%2Fgonzalez-painting-hig-res.jpg "A painting of Refugees Crossing the Border Wall into South Texas")

![Trans Forming Liberty by Amy Sherald - Smithsonian](https://npr.brightspotcdn.com/dims3/default/strip/false/crop/800x1297+0+0/resize/300/quality/85/format/webp/?url=http%3A%2F%2Fnpr-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F6a%2F83%2Feaf7a0cf4de78a6c18e4c017753c%2Fsherald-painting-smithsonian.jpg "A painting of a trans woman with pink hair and a blue gown, holding a torch in the pose of the statue of liberty")



Critics accuse the Smithsonian’s American History Museum Is [Wall-To-Wall Anti-American Propaganda](https://thefederalist.com/2025/08/15/smithsonians-american-history-museum-is-wall-to-wall-anti-american-propaganda/) and The Heritage Foundation describes it as “hothouse to curate grievances against the United States.”

[Smithsonian artists and scholars respond to White House list of objectionable art - NPR](https://www.npr.org/2025/08/24/nx-s1-5511241/smithsonian-white-house-art)
